body_txt: Embarrassed or angry, a cartoon character can go beyond a red face . The deep redness wells up from the neck and culminates in a puff of steam erupting from both ears, the top of the head, or an indeterminate point on the face. It's often accompanied by the sound of a tea kettle , factory whistle or steam locomotive whistle going off, depending on how extreme the emotion is, with more aggressive-sounding noises typically reserved for when a character's really mad . The visual effect is similar to an old thermometer rising so high, it blows its top. The Shrinking Violet is prone to this when in the presence of her crush . Resembles, but should not be confused with, some manifestations of the Fire-Breathing Diner or some Shades of Suffocation . Compare Burning with Anger and Wild Take , both of which can incorporate this trope in their presentation. For when it is thinking hard that causes smoke, see Brain Smoke .
